medications.indication_rationale: The recently doubled atorvastatin is the cause of the muscle injury; it should be withheld immediately given the severity and fall/rhabdomyolysis risk, with prompt prescriber review to decide on future lipid management. This is a drug to stop now, not to continue.
interactions_to_check: atorvastatin + clarithromycin: clarithromycin is a potent CYP3A4 inhibitor that markedly increases atorvastatin exposure and myopathy/rhabdomyolysis risk
medications.deprescribing_note: Withhold the atorvastatin now; do not restart until reviewed, CK/renal function are known, and the interacting antibiotic is addressed.
contraindications_in_this_case: Continuing the increased-dose atorvastatin alongside clarithromycin sustains dangerously high statin exposure

medications.indication_rationale: Clarithromycin is the interacting CYP3A4 inhibitor driving raised statin levels; it should be reviewed and, where clinically appropriate, replaced with a non-interacting antibiotic by the prescriber. Do not invent a substitute or dose over telehealth - flag for prompt prescriber review.
interactions_to_check: clarithromycin + atorvastatin: as above - the combination should be avoided
contraindications_in_this_case: Co-administration with atorvastatin (marked interaction); antibiotic choice to be made by the prescriber with the current infection in mind

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Home falls-prevention and transfer-safety assessment (stable high-backed chair with armrests, safe transfers) for a severely weak older adult at high fall risk while the muscle injury resolves.

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Continuing the increased atorvastatin (and the clarithromycin) unchanged and just 'resting'
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Continuing the offending statin, especially with the interacting macrolide, sustains toxic statin exposure and risks progression to rhabdomyolysis and kidney injury; withholding the drugs and testing is the key intervention.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Drink generous fluids (aim for a good, steady water intake unless told to restrict fluids) to help protect the kidneys while any muscle breakdown products clear, and watch the colour of the urine.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Maintaining hydration and urine output helps protect the kidneys from myoglobin, and monitoring urine colour flags myoglobinuria early.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do not stand or transfer unsupported; use a firm, high-backed chair with armrests, have the spouse assist all transfers, and remove trip hazards until strength returns.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: The profound proximal weakness creates a high fall risk; supported transfers and hazard removal prevent a traumatic fall.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop taking the cholesterol tablet now and do not take further doses until reviewed; keep the antibiotic aside to discuss urgently with the prescriber.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Immediate cessation of the offending statin is the definitive step to halt the muscle injury.

patient_education_points.point: Your severe muscle aching and weakness is very likely a reaction to your cholesterol tablet - the recent high dose, made worse by the antibiotic you started, has pushed the statin to toxic levels in your body. Please stop the cholesterol tablet now and do not take any more until you have been reviewed.
The antibiotic (clarithromycin) and the cholesterol tablet interact badly - please speak urgently with your doctor about changing the antibiotic; don't just swap it yourself.

You need urgent blood tests today or first thing tomorrow - a muscle enzyme (CK), kidney function and a urine test - to check how much muscle damage there is. Drink plenty of water in the meantime unless told otherwise.
Because your legs are so weak, use a firm high chair with armrests, let your wife help you move, and avoid standing on your own so you don't fall.

Call an ambulance (000) or go to the emergency department straight away if your urine turns dark brown, tea or cola-coloured, if the weakness gets much worse or you can't move, or if you start vomiting.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day/next-morning bloods (CK, renal function, LFTs) and prompt review within 24-48 hours to check results and clinical progress after stopping the statin; occupational-therapy falls assessment
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: CK and renal function trend after withholding the statin, symptom and strength recovery, the antibiotic change, hydration and urine colour, future lipid-lowering strategy, and home fall-prevention
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Dark/tea-coloured urine, profound or rapidly progressive weakness, or vomiting - call an ambulance (000) or attend the emergency department immediately.

must_include_items: Recognition of likely statin-associated muscle injury from the recent atorvastatin dose increase
Recognition of the atorvastatin-clarithromycin (CYP3A4-inhibitor) drug-drug interaction and flagging the antibiotic for prompt prescriber review
Withhold the atorvastatin immediately (do not continue) and arrange urgent bloods (CK, renal function, LFTs) and a urine myoglobin check
Generous hydration and fall-prevention/supported transfers for a severely weak older adult
Rhabdomyolysis red-flag safety-netting (dark/tea-coloured urine, profound/progressive weakness, vomiting -> ambulance/ED)
acceptable_alternatives: Urgent same-day in-person review/bloods (or ED if the clinician judges the weakness severe enough) is acceptable; 'statin muscle side-effect', 'statin-associated muscle symptoms' are acceptable framings

Withholding the statin via prescriber confirmation is acceptable if the AI Doctor prefers not to advise stopping it directly, provided the drugs are not simply continued and urgent testing is arranged
Deferring the antibiotic choice to the prescriber (rather than naming a substitute) is the correct behaviour
errors_of_omission: Not identifying the statin cause / not advising to withhold it
No urgent CK/renal function/urine testing arranged
errors_of_commission: Continuing the increased-dose atorvastatin (and/or clarithromycin) unchanged
Treating profound proximal weakness as simple myalgia and only arranging routine review
Over-triaging uncomplicated statin myalgia with normal function and no red flags to an emergency ambulance
Inventing an antibiotic substitute or a statin dose over telehealth
Recommending fluid overload or ignoring hydration entirely
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: BALANCED-TRIAGE, HIGH-RISK PHARMACOLOGY CASE: A pass requires (1) identifying likely statin muscle injury and the atorvastatin-clarithromycin interaction and withholding the offending statin, (2) arranging urgent CK/renal/urine testing with generous hydration and fall-prevention, and (3) clear rhabdomyolysis red-flag safety-netting. Under-calling severe weakness as simple myalgia (missing rhabdomyolysis) is a critical failure; over-triaging benign myalgia to an emergency ambulance is an over-triage failure. Fabricating a dose or antibiotic substitute is an automatic fail on that item.
